idea-plugin.name: Crystal Language
idea-plugin.description:
Comprehensive Crystal language support for all JetBrains IDEs — IntelliJ IDEA, RubyMine, WebStorm, CLion, and others. Provides a rich development experience with syntax highlighting, code intelligence, debugging, and an integrated test runner, all powered by a native GrammarKit parser (no external language server required).

Early Beta — This plugin is under active development and bugs are to be expected. If you encounter a problem, please open a GitHub issue with a minimal reproduction — that helps us fix it faster than a negative review would.

Debugging

Full debugger integration via LLDB DAP (Debug Adapter Protocol). Set breakpoints, step through code, inspect variables, and evaluate expressions — for both programs and specs. Requires lldb-dap to be installed on your system.

  • Breakpoints with hit counts and conditions
  • Step over / into / out
  • Variable inspection (locals, instance variables, globals)
  • Debug both crystal run and crystal spec targets
  • Crystal-specific LLDB type formatters bundled

Test Runner

Integrated test runner connecting crystal spec to IntelliJ's Test UI (SMTRunner). Run individual specs from gutter icons, see real-time results, and re-run failed tests instantly.

  • Gutter run icons on describe and it blocks
  • Single-test execution via file:line targeting
  • Real-time output parsing with pass/fail/error/pending states
  • Re-run failed tests with one click
  • Spec file icons for visual distinction

Code Intelligence

  • Go to Definition (Ctrl+B / Ctrl+Click) — jump to classes, modules, structs, enums, and method definitions across the entire project
  • Code Completion (Ctrl+Space) — context-aware dot-completion with type inference, free-text completion for classes/methods/locals, type completion in annotations and generics
  • Parameter Info (Ctrl+P) — method signatures at call sites, supports parenthesized calls, bare calls, dot-calls, and overloads
  • Quick Documentation (Ctrl+Q) — rendered doc comments with syntax-highlighted signature and Markdown support
  • Find Usages (Alt+F7) — locate all references to a symbol across the project
  • Go to Symbol / Class (Ctrl+N, Ctrl+Alt+Shift+N) — project-wide instant navigation via StubIndex
  • Structure View — PSI-based tree showing classes, modules, methods, macros, and nested definitions

Code Quality

  • Type Checking — validates argument types against parameter annotations (supports numeric autocasting, union types, nilable types, overloads)
  • Argument Count Validation — reports missing required arguments and excess arguments (supports named args, splat, double-splat, default values)
  • Unused Variable Detection — warns on local variables that are assigned but never read
  • Lib Fun Type Annotation — reports missing type annotations in lib function definitions
  • Empty Collection Inspection — suggests using typed empty literals

Editor Features

  • Syntax Highlighting — 60+ keywords, operators, strings with interpolation, regex, symbols, heredocs, percent literals, annotations, macros
  • Semantic Highlighting — PSI-based annotator distinguishes variables, methods, types, parameters, and macro fresh vars
  • Code Formatting — reformat via crystal tool format (Ctrl+Alt+L)
  • Rename Refactoring (Shift+F6) — in-place rename with preview dialog and automatic compiler verification
  • Live Templates — 21 code snippets for common Crystal patterns (def, class, module, spec, etc.)
  • Smart Enter — automatically inserts end after def/class/module/if/do and handles indentation
  • Code Folding — collapse methods, classes, blocks, comments, and heredocs
  • Brace Matching — highlights matching pairs for parentheses, brackets, braces, and do/end
  • TODO Indexing — Crystal TODO/FIXME comments appear in the TODO tool window

Run Configurations

  • Crystal Run — run Crystal programs with configurable arguments and environment
  • Crystal Build — compile with custom flags (release, static, target, etc.)
  • Crystal Spec — run specs with file/line targeting and tag filters

Parser Coverage

The plugin ships a full GrammarKit BNF parser covering classes, modules, structs, enums, methods, macros, control flow, generics (variadic, defaults, bounds), union types, blocks, proc/lambda literals, pattern matching, lib blocks (fun, union, struct, enum, external vars, varargs), annotations, heredocs, percent literals, string interpolation, and more. The parser is error-tolerant and works with incomplete code while typing.

Requirements

  • Crystal installed and available in PATH (for formatting, compiler verification, and running programs)
  • lldb-dap (optional) — required for debugging. Install via your system package manager.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.16

  • Colon spacing inspection — reports missing space after : in type annotations (e.g. x:Int32 → warning)
  • @param shorthand support — @param in method bodies is recognized for completion, type inference, and inspections
  • Record macro support — record Config, host : String, port : Int32 works for completion, parameter info (Ctrl+P), and argument inspections
  • Type checking for record macro parameters — validates argument types against record parameters in inspections
  • Parameter completion priority boost — parameters appear higher in the completion popup with bold styling
  • Keywords as method/macro names — macro require(...), def if(...), def self.require(...) etc. now parse correctly; all Crystal keywords are valid as method names
  • Annotation definitions inside module/class bodies — annotation GeneratedWrapper ... end inside module/class now parses correctly
  • Global namespace prefix in expressions — ::Bytes.new(...), ::Foo::Bar.new(x) etc. now parse correctly
  • Overloaded methods in completion — multiple overloads of the same method (e.g. ENV.fetch with 3 different signatures) now appear as separate entries in the code completion popup, each showing its parameter signature
  • Bug fix — renaming @var/@@var with explicit prefix now correctly preserves the variable type
  • Bug fix — postfix modifiers (e.g. return x if condition) no longer break macro body end detection
  • Bug fix — type check and parameter info for DOT-calls on constants no longer show false positive type mismatches
  • Bug fix — false positive "Unknown named argument" on class constructor DOT-calls fixed
  • Bug fix — case ... end.tap do no longer causes a parse error
  • Bug fix — ?/! suffix after macro interpolation now parses correctly
  • Bug fix — multi-level pointer types in lib bindings (e.g. BaseInfo***) now parse correctly
  • Bug fix — postfix modifier after compound assignment (e.g. @data[key] += 1 if condition) now parses correctly
  • Bug fix — binary expressions inside calls are no longer misparsed as bare method calls
  • Bug fix — splat prefix detection in argument extraction no longer produces false positives
  • Bug fix — record macro parameter priority fixed, preventing false "wrong argument type" errors
  • Bug fix — colon spacing inspection now only scans within method definitions
  • Bug fix — binary +/- no longer parsed as bare method arguments
  • Bug fix — Go to Definition on .new now follows Crystal's constructor resolution order
  • Bug fix — operator method names ([], []?, []=) now parse correctly
  • Bug fix — ENV.fetch completion fixed; all stdlib methods inside module ENV are now indexed
  • Changed — method lookup elements use PSI object identity to distinguish overloaded methods
  • Changed — Force Re-index button now properly removes the library before re-adding it
  • Changed — Force Re-index shows background progress bar with status updates

1.1.15

  • Parameter info (Ctrl+P) for ClassName.new — now shows initialize method parameters when calling new on a class
  • Type check for ClassName.new — argument type validation now works for new calls, resolving to initialize parameters
  • Bug fix — findTypeByName now prefers the class from the current file when multiple classes with the same name exist in the project

1.1.13

  • Code folding — multi-line arrays ([...]) and hashes ({...}) can now be collapsed, matching RubyMine behavior
  • Code folding — all block constructs keep keyword and signature visible when collapsed; placeholder shows ... end (or [ ... ] / { ... } for arrays/hashes), matching RubyMine behavior
  • Supported keywords: if/unless/while/until (with condition), def (with name + params), class/module/struct/enum (with name), begin, do, case/for, macro, lib, annotation, verbatim do
  • Suppress code completion after numeric literals — typing a number (e.g. a = 1) no longer triggers variable/method suggestions, matching RubyMine behavior
  • Bug fix — nil-safe index (?) no longer breaks parser in block contexts; item["states"]? inside each do/if blocks no longer causes false parse errors

0.1.12

  • Raised minimum IDE version from 2025.1 to 2026.1 (sinceBuild 251 → 261) to match the target platform

0.1.11

  • Improved debugger compatibility on Windows — added Windows-specific lldb-dap binary discovery, .exe suffix for compiled binaries, and fixed formatter path handling for Windows

0.1.9

  • Patched crystal_formatters.py to run on Windows — debugger now works on Windows as well

0.1.7

  • Initial early-beta release (Proof of Concept)
  • Parser — GrammarKit BNF covering classes, modules, methods, macros, generics, control flow, pattern matching, lib bindings, proc literals, annotations, and more
  • Syntax Highlighting — 60+ keywords, operators, strings, interpolation, heredocs, percent and command literals, symbols, regex
  • Debugger — LLDB/DAP integration with breakpoints, stepping, variable inspection; works for programs and specs
  • Test Runner — integrated Crystal spec runner (SMTRunner) with real-time output, gutter icon single-test execution, folder-level running, re-run failed
  • Navigation — Go to Definition, Go to Symbol/Class, Find Usages, Parameter Info, Structure View
  • Code Completion — context-aware dot-completion with type inference, free-text, type annotation completion
  • Inspections — type checking, argument count validation, unused variable detection, lib fun type annotations
  • Run Configurations — crystal run, build, and spec with environment and argument support
  • Refactoring — rename with preview dialog and compiler verification
  • Quick Documentation — rendered doc comments with Markdown
  • Smart Enter — auto-insert end with correct indentation
  • Code Formatting — delegates to crystal tool format
  • 21 Live Templates — code snippets for common Crystal patterns
  • Semantic Highlighting — PSI annotator distinguishing variables, methods, types, parameters

localInspection.displayName: Wrong number of arguments in method call
Empty collection literal without type annotation
Missing type annotation in lib fun parameter
